title: Reading - Part 3
instructions: Read the article and choose the correct answer (A, B or C) for each question.
article:
Last year I decided to try joining a local sports club. At first, I wasn’t completely sure it was the right thing for me. 
A friend had mentioned it several times and eventually persuaded me to give it a chance. 
I expected it to be interesting, but I also worried that I might not be good enough.

The first few weeks were harder than I expected. Everyone else seemed more experienced and confident, 
while I kept making small mistakes. Sometimes I thought about giving up, especially after long days at school. 
However, the people around me were patient and supportive, which helped me relax and improve slowly.

One thing that really surprised me was how friendly everyone was. I thought people would be competitive, 
but instead they were happy to answer questions and share advice. That made the experience much more enjoyable 
than I had imagined before joining.

Over time, I realised I was learning much more than just the main activity itself. I became better at solving 
problems, communicating with different people and staying calm when things changed unexpectedly. These skills 
have also helped me in school and other parts of my life.

Looking back now, I’m glad I decided to try something outside my normal routine. I’d definitely encourage 
other teenagers to do the same, even if they feel uncertain at first. Sometimes the experiences that seem 
most difficult at the beginning become the most rewarding later.

questions.question: Why did the writer first become interested in the activity?

options.C: Their family asked them to help.

questions.question: What problem did the writer have at the beginning?

options.A: They didn’t have enough free time.

questions.question: What has the writer learned from the experience?
What would the writer probably say to other teenagers?

options.A: Try something new even if you feel nervous.
options.B: Wait until you have more experience.
